title: Asking About Japan's Regions
titleJa: 日本の地方について聞く
description: A learner asks a Japanese friend to describe how regions of Japan differ in climate, food, and dialect.
canDo: I can ask structured questions about regional differences in a country and follow up with comparisons.
sampleDialogue: 日本は南北に長いので、地方によって気候も文化もずいぶん違うと聞いたことがあります。具体的にはどのような違いがあるんでしょうか。
practicePrompts: Ask how the climate of Hokkaido differs from that of Okinawa, using 〜に比べて.
Ask which region's food they personally recommend and why, using 〜と言えば.
Follow up by asking whether regional dialects (方言) are still commonly used today.

title: Explaining Your Hometown's Geography
titleJa: 出身地の地理を説明する
description: The learner explains the geography and characteristics of their hometown or region to a curious Japanese partner.
canDo: I can describe the geographic features, climate, and cultural character of a region in a structured way.
sampleDialogue: あなたの出身地はどんなところですか。地理的な特徴や、その土地ならではの文化があれば、ぜひ教えてください。
practicePrompts: Describe your hometown's location and climate using 〜に位置している and 〜という特徴がある.
Compare your region's culture with a neighboring region using 〜のに対して.
Explain one local specialty (名物) and why it became famous there.

title: Preparing a Self-Introduction Speech
titleJa: 自己紹介スピーチの準備
description: The learner asks for advice on structuring a formal self-introduction speech for a Japanese audience.
canDo: I can ask for and discuss advice on how to organize a formal spoken presentation.
sampleDialogue: 今度、ゼミで自己紹介のスピーチをすることになったんですが、どのように構成すれば聞き手に印象を残せるでしょうか。
practicePrompts: Ask whether you should open with a question or with a personal story, using 〜たほうがいいでしょうか.
Ask how to politely end a speech, including 結び of gratitude.
Ask for feedback on whether your draft sounds too casual or too stiff.

title: Giving Feedback on a Classmate's Speech
titleJa: クラスメートのスピーチに意見を述べる
description: The learner gives constructive feedback after a classmate's speech, balancing praise and suggestions.
canDo: I can give structured, polite feedback on a presentation, identifying strengths and suggesting improvements.
sampleDialogue: さっきのスピーチ、内容はとても面白かったんですが、率直な感想を聞かせてもらえませんか。改善点があれば教えてほしいんです。
practicePrompts: Praise one specific aspect using 〜ところがよかった and explain why it worked.
Suggest one improvement gently using 〜ばもっとよくなると思います.
Ask a follow-up question about the topic to show genuine interest.

title: Settling Into a Homestay
titleJa: ホームステイに馴染む
description: On the first evening of a homestay, the learner asks the host family about house rules and customs.
canDo: I can politely ask about household rules and cultural expectations using appropriate formal register.
sampleDialogue: 今日からお世話になります。ご迷惑をおかけしないように、この家のルールや習慣を教えていただけませんか。
practicePrompts: Ask what time you should be home by, using 何時までに〜たらいいですか.
Ask whether there is anything you must not do, using 〜てはいけないこと.
Offer to help with chores using 〜お手伝いさせていただきます.

title: Explaining a Cultural Misunderstanding
titleJa: 文化的な誤解を説明する
description: The learner explains a small cultural misunderstanding that happened during a homestay and reflects on what they learned.
canDo: I can narrate a past intercultural experience and reflect on what it taught me.
sampleDialogue: ホームステイ中に、ちょっとした文化的な誤解があって少し気まずい思いをしたんですが、その経験から色々学ぶことができました。
practicePrompts: Describe what happened using 〜てしまった to convey regret.
Explain why the misunderstanding occurred using 〜という理由で.
Reflect on what you learned, using 〜ということに気づきました.

title: Asking About Japanese Tech Innovation
titleJa: 日本のテクノロジー革新について尋ねる
description: The learner interviews a Japanese partner about what makes Japanese technology distinctive globally.
canDo: I can interview someone about a complex topic and ask follow-up questions to deepen the discussion.
sampleDialogue: 日本の技術は世界的に評価されていますが、その強みはどこにあると思いますか。具体的な例を挙げて教えていただけませんか。
practicePrompts: Ask which industry best represents Japan's strengths, using 〜の中で.
Ask whether tradition (伝統) and technology can really coexist, using 〜ことができるのでしょうか.
Follow up by asking about a recent example they have seen in the news.

title: Discussing Robots in Daily Life
titleJa: 日常生活におけるロボット
description: The learner gives their opinion on whether robots and AI will improve or harm daily life in Japan.
canDo: I can present a balanced opinion on a technological issue, citing both benefits and concerns.
sampleDialogue: 最近、介護や接客の現場でロボットが使われ始めていますよね。便利な反面、心配な点もあると思うんですが、どう思いますか。
practicePrompts: State one clear benefit using 〜というメリットがある.
Raise a concern using 〜のではないでしょうか.
Conclude with your overall position using 結局のところ〜と思います.

title: Asking About Kabuki and Noh
titleJa: 歌舞伎と能について聞く
description: The learner asks a knowledgeable friend to introduce them to traditional Japanese performing arts.
canDo: I can ask informed questions about an unfamiliar cultural practice and request a comparative explanation.
sampleDialogue: 前から伝統芸能に興味があって、特に歌舞伎と能の違いがよくわからないんですが、それぞれの特徴を簡単に教えてもらえませんか。
practicePrompts: Ask which one is older and how it developed, using 〜はいつごろ生まれたんですか.
Ask what kind of audience traditionally watched each, using どんな人たちが.
Ask whether a beginner should start with kabuki or noh and why.

title: Explaining Why Traditions Survive
titleJa: 伝統が受け継がれる理由を説明する
description: The learner explains to a foreign friend why traditional arts continue to be valued in modern Japan.
canDo: I can explain the social and cultural reasons a tradition is preserved, using cause-and-effect expressions.
sampleDialogue: 現代でも歌舞伎や能のような伝統芸能が大切にされているのは、単に古いからではなく、ちゃんと理由があるんだと思うんですよ。
practicePrompts: Give one cultural reason using 〜という理由から.
Explain government or community support using 〜によって守られている.
Conclude with your own view on whether this will continue, using 今後も〜だろう.

title: Discussing Regional Cuisine
titleJa: 地方料理について話し合う
description: The learner asks about regional specialties and how local ingredients shape Japanese cuisine.
canDo: I can ask thoughtful questions about food culture and connect ingredients to geography and history.
sampleDialogue: 日本料理といっても地方によってずいぶん違うらしいですね。例えば、関西と関東ではどんな点が違うんでしょうか。
practicePrompts: Ask how climate influences local ingredients using 〜が原因で.
Ask about a regional specialty (郷土料理) you have heard of.
Ask whether young people still eat traditional dishes at home.

title: Introducing Your Country's Food
titleJa: 自国の食文化を紹介する
description: The learner introduces a dish from their country and explains its cultural meaning to a Japanese partner.
canDo: I can describe a dish and explain its cultural and social significance in my country.
sampleDialogue: 私の国にも、特別な日に必ず食べる料理があるんですよ。日本でいうとお正月のおせちのような存在かもしれません。
practicePrompts: Describe what the dish is and what it tastes like, using 〜のような味.
Explain when and why people eat it, using 〜ことになっている.
Compare it to a Japanese equivalent using 日本でいうと〜にあたる.

title: Asking Why Konbini Are So Popular
titleJa: コンビニの人気の理由を聞く
description: The learner asks a Japanese partner to explain why convenience stores have become central to daily life in Japan.
canDo: I can ask analytical questions about a social phenomenon and request reasons behind it.
sampleDialogue: 日本のコンビニは本当に便利だとよく言われますが、ここまで生活に欠かせない存在になったのは、どうしてだと思いますか。
practicePrompts: Ask which services surprise foreigners the most, using 外国人にとって.
Ask whether konbini have any downsides, using マイナス面.
Follow up by asking how konbini might change in the future.

title: Explaining Japanese Service Culture
titleJa: 日本のサービス文化を説明する
description: The learner explains their impression of Japanese omotenashi and customer service to a friend back home.
canDo: I can describe a cultural concept and give specific examples to illustrate it.
sampleDialogue: 日本のサービス、いわゆる「おもてなし」って、ただ丁寧なだけじゃなくて、相手の気持ちを先回りして考えるところがすごいと感じています。
practicePrompts: Give one concrete example you experienced, using 〜たことがあるんですが.
Explain how it differs from service in your country, using 〜と違って.
Reflect on whether this level of service is sustainable for workers.

title: Asking About Sumo and Baseball
titleJa: 相撲と野球について聞く
description: The learner asks why both a traditional sport and a foreign-origin sport became deeply popular in Japan.
canDo: I can ask comparative questions about cultural phenomena and request historical context.
sampleDialogue: 日本では相撲のような伝統的なスポーツと、野球のような外国から来たスポーツが両方人気ですよね。これにはどんな背景があるんでしょうか。
practicePrompts: Ask when baseball first became popular in Japan, using いつごろから.
Ask what makes sumo different from other martial arts, using 〜と比べて.
Ask which sport young people today are most interested in.

title: Discussing Sports in Daily Life
titleJa: 日常生活におけるスポーツ
description: The learner shares their view on the role of sports and exercise in modern Japanese society.
canDo: I can express a structured opinion about the role of sport in society, with reasons and examples.
sampleDialogue: 最近、健康のためにスポーツを始める社会人が増えているそうですが、忙しい日本の生活の中で運動を続けるのは簡単ではないと思うんです。
practicePrompts: State why exercise matters using 〜にとって欠かせない.
Give a reason it's hard to keep up, using 〜にもかかわらず.
Suggest one realistic solution using 〜たらどうでしょうか.
